title: Training Module - Microscopy
summary: Use fluorescence microscopy to identify organelles and proteins in three different experimental samples by observing their specific staining colors.

key_factors.why: These are the unknown samples that must be identified based on their organelle/protein staining patterns.
Different organelles and proteins are labeled with specific fluorescent tags requiring these specific channels to be enabled for visualization.
These known organelle and protein images serve as reference structures to identify the staining patterns in the samples.

sequence.step: Set up and prepare slides for Sample 1, Sample 2, and Sample 3.
sequence.why: Required to load the samples into the microscope for observation.

sequence.step: Enable the required fluorescence channels (blue, green, red, and orange) and turn on the laser.
sequence.why: Necessary to excite the fluorescent labels and capture images of the organelles and proteins.

sequence.step: Image the reference organelles/proteins and the experimental samples 1, 2, and 3.
sequence.why: Allows for the comparison of unknown sample structures against known reference labels to perform identification.
